---
title: "Can't Use Alt-Gr"
date: 2005-04-04
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by bigblop on 2005-04-04
I use a danish keyboard. For some reason I cannot type any chars that depend on Alt-Gr (backslash, pipe, tilde, AT etc.). Wher in Ubuntu is it possible to change keyboard layout or is it only possible to use Ubuntu if you have an english keyboard??

This is how my XF86Config-4 file looks like:



# XF86Config-4 (XFree86 X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the XF86Config-4 manual page.

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"

EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Silicon Integrated Systems (SiS) SG86C202"

	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync	30-110
	VertRefresh	50-160
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

### Post by erkki70 on 2005-04-05
Hi,
Backup your XF86config file and try to change the InputDevice section for your keyboard to this:
```
Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier "Keyboard1"
    Driver "keyboard"
    Option "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option "XkbLayout" "dk"
    Option "XkbOptions" ""
EndSection
```

This "should" work fine.
In case you want to configure more keys, special keys you need to search these forums with:
"xmodmap". You'll find what you need.

Do you run your system in Danish?
Are your Locales set to da_DK?
Have you set your keyboard to Danish within Gnome or KDE or your favourite WM?

Hope this helps.  :wink: 

Cheers,
Erkki

---
